summary:
Held 0 of today's 10 biggest UK/EU movers.

• 6 upside misses — ICG.L +3%, DSFIR.AS +3%, MRK.DE +3%, IMCD.AS +3%, III.L +3%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 4 downside moves (AUTO.L -8%, CTEC.L -5%, CBK.DE -3%, AIR.PA -3%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: AUTO.L (-7.94%) — FY26 revenue +4% to £624.3m broadly in line, but mgmt flagged a lower FY2027 ARPR starting run rate and elevated Deal Builder cancellations; FY27 op-profit guidance of £395–415m disappointed vs consensus. Why we passed: Outside any active strategy's universe. AUTO.L is a FTSE 100 large-cap UK online automotive marketplace; the ~8% drop was a post-earnings guidance-miss gap requiring pre-open positioning on results day before price discovery at the open.

top_movers.catalyst: ICG released FY2026 final results on 21 May: $17B fundraising beat forecasts, FRE +23% to £350M, record operating cash flow of £861M, and a 16th consecutive ordinary dividend increase.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. ICG.L is a FTSE 100 alternative asset manager whose +3.44% move was driven by a scheduled full-year earnings release — a catalyst-type event requiring pre-announcement universe inclusion, not a real-time signal.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a pre-earnings expansion pass: any FTSE 100 name with a confirmed results date within 2 calendar days should be admitted to the news-reactive universe; enter on gap ≥+2% with volume ≥1.2× 20-day avg in the first 30 minutes.

top_movers.catalyst: DSM-Firmenich begins dual listing on SIX Swiss Exchange effective 2026-05-21, alongside a €540M share buyback program, broadening investor access and signalling capital returns.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe; DSM-Firmenich is a large-cap (~€25B mkt cap) Euronext Amsterdam materials/nutrition name. Today's move was a scheduled corporate-action catalyst (new exchange listing + buyback launch) invisible to intraday momentum or mean-reversion signals.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a corporate-action pre-filter ingesting dual-listing effective dates and buyback-initiation filings ≥48h ahead; place qualifying names on a day-of watchlist for the catalyst-gap strategy with a dedicated position-size cap.

top_movers.catalyst: Phase 3 TroFuse-005 trial (announced May 18) showed sacituzumab tirumotecan met dual OS+PFS endpoints in advanced endometrial cancer — first TROP2 ADC to achieve this; market continued re-rating the oncology pipeline on May 21.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. MRK.DE is a DAX 40 diversified conglomerate not covered by sector filters; the catalyst was a binary Phase 3 readout on May 18 requiring pre-announcement positioning — May 21 was 3-day continuation drift.
top_movers.suggestion: Add DAX large-cap pharma names to a catalyst-continuation universe; trigger entries on confirmed Phase 3 OS+PFS beats with ≥+2% cumulative drift within 3 sessions post-announcement and volume ≥1.3× 20-day avg.

top_movers.catalyst: No company-specific news found for May 21; likely technical breakout above key €94 resistance with European specialty-chemicals sector rotation — Jefferies raised PT €108→€122 on May 11 and Goldman reaffirmed Buy May 14.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — IMCD is an AEX-listed mid-large-cap specialty chemicals distributor not in current sector filters. Move appears technical/sector-driven with no pre-market news signal to trigger a reactive strategy.
top_movers.suggestion: Add AEX-listed specialty chemicals distributors to a momentum universe with a breakout filter (close > 20-day resistance + volume ≥1.2× avg) to capture sector-rotation moves; requires no new short capability.

top_movers.catalyst: Post-FY2026-results recovery: stock fell ~19% on May 14 on Action's LFL slowdown to 2.4%; the concurrent £750m buyback (first in 21 yrs) drove active repurchases at £18.38–£22.42, underpinning a multi-session rebound.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe: 3i is a FTSE 100 closed-end PE/investment company (diversified financials), excluded from operating-company strategies. Move is also a delayed post-results bounce from May 14 selloff — misses same-day signal filters.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-results bounce screen: FTSE 100 investment companies down >10% on results day with a concurrent buyback ≥£500m should be queued for mean-reversion entry within 5 sessions, capped at 1% NAV exposure per name.

top_movers.catalyst: Babcock's May 20 announcement of a new 10-supplier strategic supply chain framework for its defence portfolio reignited sentiment; stock had fallen ~24% since £200M buyback completion at 1094p on April 28.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. Babcock (~£5B UK defence/engineering) was in a sharp post-buyback downtrend that would disqualify it from momentum filters; catching the bounce required pre-open ingestion of the May 20 RNS announcement.
top_movers.suggestion: Extend the UK mid-cap industrials universe to include FTSE 250 defence names (BAB.L, QinetiQ); add a news-scan gate on LSE RNS releases so contract/partnership announcements flag eligible entries before market open the following session.

top_movers.catalyst: FY26 revenue +4% to £624.3m broadly in line, but mgmt flagged a lower FY2027 ARPR starting run rate and elevated Deal Builder cancellations; FY27 op-profit guidance of £395–415m disappointed vs consensus.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. AUTO.L is a FTSE 100 large-cap UK online automotive marketplace; the ~8% drop was a post-earnings guidance-miss gap requiring pre-open positioning on results day before price discovery at the open.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a FTSE 100 earnings-event rule: on results day, flag names where FY+1 op-profit guidance midpoint trails prior consensus by ≥3% and intraday open gap is ≥5%; route to a gap-fade or short overlay at open if short capability exists.

top_movers.catalyst: Pre-market RNS AGM trading update revealed InnovaMatrix revenues down ~24% YoY (FY guidance cut to 'at least $75m' from $99m prior year) plus caution on Medicare reimbursement uncertainty for Advanced Wound Care.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe. CTEC.L is a large-cap FTSE 100 medical-devices name (~£3-4bn mkt cap); the catalyst was a pre-market RNS guidance cut requiring pre-open short positioning that post-open momentum and news-reactive filters arrive too late to act on.
top_movers.suggestion: Add FTSE 100 healthcare names to news-reactive universe with a pre-market RNS scanner flagging guidance-cut keywords ('at least $X', product revenue below prior-year actuals); trigger short entry within 15 min of open on gap-down ≥2% — requires short capability, else honest no-op.

top_movers.catalyst: CBK.DE went ex-dividend on 2026-05-21 (EUR 1.10/share approved at May 20 AGM); the ~€1.10 dividend strip mechanically accounts for virtually all of the −3.18% close-to-close decline.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — CBK.DE is a DAX large-cap German bank (~€23B market cap) in a sector likely excluded by the active strategies. Independently, the move is a mechanical dividend-ex adjustment, not a momentum or mean-reversion signal, so no technical filter would have produced a valid entry.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an ex-dividend adjustment to the missed-movers screen: subtract the declared dividend from close-to-close return on ex-date before ranking, so mechanical payout strips don't surface as apparent gap candidates and pollute the signal list.

top_movers.catalyst: Airbus warned airlines of further A350 delivery delays due to production bottlenecks at its ex-Spirit AeroSystems Kinston NC fuselage plant; Paris Court of Appeal also convicted Airbus over AF447 crash same day.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — AIR.PA is a mega-cap CAC 40 industrial excluded by the bot's cap/sector filters. Both catalysts broke pre-open, favouring pre-positioned shorts over reactive intraday signals.
top_movers.suggestion: Expand the news-reactive strategy's universe to include CAC 40 large-cap industrials and add a supply-chain-warning event trigger (≥2% implied gap at open) to allow same-day short entries on delivery-delay headlines.
